disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Essentially zero; one of the highest cancer mortality rates; 5-year survival ~12% overall; only 15-20% have resectable disease at diagnosis
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Resectable: surgery (Whipple) + FOLFIRINOX adjuvant: 54.4 months median OS (PRODIGE 24, vs 35.0 months gemcitabine); BRCA-mutant metastatic: olaparib maintenance after platinum-based: 7.4 vs 3.8 months PFS (POLO); neoadjuvant chemotherapy: may convert 20-30% of borderline-resectable to resectable
remission.gap_size: Very large — minimal survival improvement for most patients over past 20 years; imaging and liquid biopsy for early detection is the biggest opportunity
remission.barrier_detail: Pancreatic cancer is detected late (70-80% at Stage III-IV) due to absence of early symptoms and screening; CA19-9 is insufficiently sensitive/specific for early detection; BRCA1/2 and other HRR mutation testing is not universal; KRAS G12D/G12V targeted agents represent a genuine pipeline advance
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — NALIRIFOX first-line for metastatic PDAC (NAPOLI-3 — modest improvement); 2020 olaparib maintenance for BRCA-mutant; 2019 FOLFIRINOX adjuvant standard; gemcitabine + nab-paclitaxel first-line since 2013; gemcitabine monotherapy since 1997
remission.outdated_treatments: 5-FU monotherapy (replaced by gemcitabine since 1997, yet still seen as palliative option); biliary stent without concurrent systemic therapy in borderline-resectable (multidisciplinary evaluation mandatory before stenting in surgically eligible patients); COX-2 inhibitor chemoprevention (observational data; not proven in RCTs)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Weight maintenance and muscle preservation during chemotherapy (sarcopenia worsens OS and treatment tolerance); palliative care early integration (improves QoL and OS in metastatic PDAC — parallel to lung cancer data); CA-19-9 monitoring combined with ctDNA for response assessment
remission.frontier_signal: KRAS G12D/G12V inhibitors (MRTX1133 — Phase 1/2 underway; KRAS mutant in 90% of PDAC); RAS-targeted therapies expanding from lung (sotorasib/adagrasib for G12C, <1% PDAC); mRNA personalised cancer vaccines (mRNA-4157 Phase 2 data in resected PDAC — V940-001); claudin 18.2-targeting (zolbetuximab — Phase 3 SPOTLIGHT/GLOW data extending to PDAC); immune-suppressive stroma targeting approaches
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
